title: thymus serpyllum var. trachselianus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. trachselianus, commonly known as Wild Thyme, is a low-growing, flowering perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Wild Thyme include its ability to soothe coughs and colds, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy. Wild Thyme can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be added to salves and ointments to enhance their therapeutic properties.
